title: Outcome Measurement, Baselines, Guardrails, and Evaluation Design · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs outcome measurement, baselines, guardrails, and evaluation design from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for outcome evaluation and measurement protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.
Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.
Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change
steps.instruction:
Order the Theory Of Change evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Theory Of Change assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change, keep Theory Of Change connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Theory Of Change has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Theory Of Change precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.

For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Theory Of Change, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A feature roadmap is equivalent to a theory of change.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the review Theory Of Change case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the review Theory Of Change case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the review Theory Of Change case

stimulus.title: Analysis Precommitment case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Changing an analysis after seeing results is harmless exploration.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data. Artifact under review: outcome evaluation and measurement protocol.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Analysis Precommitment, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Analysis Precommitment
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Analysis Precommitment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Analysis Precommitment.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Analysis Precommitment, keep Analysis Precommitment connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review Analysis Precommitment, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Analysis Precommitment precondition, or invariant that must hold.

For Record what required effort · Review Analysis Precommitment, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Changing an analysis after seeing results is harmless exploration.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Theory Of Change to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Theory Of Change.

checks.description: The decision applies Operational Measure to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Operational Measure.

checks.description: The decision applies Baseline Comparison to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Baseline Comparison.

checks.description: The decision applies Guardrail Equity to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Guardrail Equity.

checks.description: The decision applies Analysis Precommitment to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Analysis Precommitment.

checks.description: The decision applies Analysis Precommitment to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Analysis Precommitment.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using precommit and inclusion plus specific case evidence.
